calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-27-2009 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-13-2009 Continued To Apr-27-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Henry Shain for Alice Christy (Objector/Beneficiary). Steven Weinkauf for the San Francisco Public Administrator ("SFPA").

Mr. Weinkauf stated that he did not have the tax certificate, that the Investigator for the SFPA contacted the Franchise Tax Board on February 13th to expedite the matter and that he would contact the Decedent's tax unit from the Franchise Tax Board. Mr. Shain requested a preliminary distribution in the amount of $20,000.00. Mr. Wienkauf stated that he would not object to a $17,000.00 preliminary distribution and requested a preliminary distribution of the property. The Court took the issues regarding the preliminary distribution under submission. Mr. Shain will prepare the order leaving the dollar amount blank. The order is to include the property and is to be circulated to Mr. Weinkauf for approval as to form. The matter was continued to April 27, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Svahlin.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-30-2009 Continued To Apr-13-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Henry Shain for Alice Christy.

Mr. Shain stated that they were ready to go through with their contract, that their fees should be surcharged or reduced, noted that the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA) should not get an extra salary for a salaried employee, that the SFPA cost his client thousands of dollars, that the SFPA didn't show up for court today, that all of this is wrong, and that the SFPA's opposition admits their failings. The Court granted the petition subject to the SFPA filing an income tax certificate by April 13, 2009. Status on the income tax certificate by the SFPA was set for April 13, 2009; once that is on file the Court stated it would sign the order for final distribution.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-05-2008 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Henry Shain for Alice Christy (Petitioner). Steven Weinkauf for the San Francisco Public Administrator.

Mr. Shain argued that Ms. Christy had highest priority as she was the only heir, that she was unable to get any responses from the Administrator, that there was no opposition declaration and that she should become the Administator. Mr. Weinkauff stated that there was no petition for removal, that he had copies of e-mail exchange, that he had been cooperating and that the estate was ready to be closed. The Court stated that a petition for removal needed to be filed and directed Mr. Weinkauf to provide proof of everything he had done to Mr. Shain. The Petition For Appointment of Successor Administrator was taken off calendar.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. Neu.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-29-2008 Continued To Aug-05-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Henry Shain for Alice Christy (Petitioner).

Mr. Weinkauf was not present in Court. Mr. Shain stated that he had not heard from Mr. Weinkauf and that there was no oppostion. The matter was continued to August 5, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Leticia Perez CSR#7782. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-09-2008 Continued To Jul-29-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
